meaning: n.溢出,(大城市容纳不下的)外流人口
examples: A second room was needed for the spillover of staff and reporters.
Some jobs are quite likely to have a negative spillover into family life.
But office romances can have a negative spillover effect on co-workers.
The U.S. is particularly concerned about possible spillover of fighting into Jordan and Iraq.
The bigger concern than reputation cross-contamination is the spillover of an actual security threat.
